instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, big toes touching if comfortable.
Distribute weight evenly across all four corners of each foot.
Engage thighs, lift kneecaps, and lengthen the tailbone toward the floor.
Roll shoulders back and down; let arms hang naturally beside the body, palms facing forward.
Lengthen the crown of the head toward the ceiling. Breathe steadily.
benefits: Establishes a grounded foundation for all standing poses
contraindications: Low blood pressure (avoid standing still for long periods)
Insomnia (some practitioners find it stimulating before sleep)

instructions: Begin in Mountain Pose.
On an inhale, sweep both arms out and overhead.
Bring palms to touch or keep them shoulder-width apart, biceps by the ears.
Lift through the side waist and gaze gently up between the thumbs.
benefits: Stretches the entire side body, shoulders, and armpits
contraindications: Neck pain (keep gaze forward instead of up)

instructions: Stand with feet together or hip-width apart.
Inhale and raise arms overhead, palms facing each other.
Exhale and bend the knees, sinking the hips back as if sitting in a chair.
Keep weight in the heels; lengthen the tailbone down to protect the lower back.
Hold steady and breathe.
benefits: Strengthens thighs, ankles, calves, and spine
Stimulates abdominal organs and the heart

instructions: From Mountain, step the left foot back about 4 feet, angling the back foot 45 degrees.
Square the hips toward the front of the mat.
Bend the front knee to 90 degrees, tracking over the ankle.
Sweep the arms overhead, palms facing each other, gaze forward or up.
Repeat on the other side.
benefits: Strengthens shoulders, arms, legs, ankles, and back
contraindications: Shoulder issues (keep arms parallel or hands on hips)

instructions: Stand with feet 4 to 4.5 feet apart, arms extended at shoulder height.
Turn the right foot out 90 degrees and the left foot in slightly.
Bend the right knee directly over the ankle until the thigh is parallel to the floor.
Reach actively through both fingertips; gaze over the front hand.
Hold, then repeat on the second side.
benefits: Strengthens and stretches legs and ankles
contraindications: Neck issues (look forward instead of over the hand)

instructions: From Warrior I or High Lunge, shift weight into the front foot.
Lift the back leg parallel to the floor as you hinge the torso forward.
Form a long line from fingertips to back heel; flex the lifted foot.
Engage the core and standing leg; gaze a few inches in front of you.
Switch sides.
benefits: Strengthens ankles, legs, shoulders, and back muscles

instructions: Stand with feet 3.5 to 4 feet apart. Turn the right foot out 90 degrees, left foot in slightly.
Extend arms parallel to the floor at shoulder height.
Hinge from the front hip and reach the right hand forward, then down to the shin, ankle, or block.
Stack the top shoulder over the bottom and reach the left hand to the ceiling.
Gaze up at the top hand. Repeat on the second side.
benefits: Stretches hips, hamstrings, calves, and spine
contraindications: Neck injury (look forward instead of up)

instructions: From Warrior II on the right side, bring the right forearm to the right thigh, or fingertips to the floor outside the right foot.
Extend the left arm overhead, alongside the ear, creating a long diagonal line from heel to fingertips.
Open the chest toward the ceiling and gaze under the top arm.
Hold and breathe; repeat on the second side.
benefits: Strengthens and stretches the legs, knees, and ankles
Opens the side body, chest, lungs, and shoulders

instructions: From a forward fold, step the left foot back into a long lunge with the back leg straight and heel lifted.
Bend the front knee to 90 degrees, stacked over the ankle.
Sweep the arms overhead, palms facing each other.
Lengthen the tailbone toward the back heel; lift the chest.
Switch sides.
contraindications: Knee injury (lower the back knee for Low Lunge)

instructions: From Downward Dog, step the right foot between the hands and lower the left knee to the floor.
Untuck the back toes; sink the hips forward and down.
Inhale and lift the torso, sweeping the arms overhead.
Soften the front of the back hip toward the mat; lift through the heart.
Switch sides.
benefits: Opens the chest, shoulders, and groin
contraindications: Knee injury (pad under the back knee)
Heart conditions (keep hands at heart center)

instructions: From High Lunge with the right leg forward, lower the back knee briefly to set up if needed.
Bring the hands to the heart and twist to the right, hooking the left elbow outside the right thigh.
Press palms together firmly; press the back leg straight, lifting through the back heel.
Open the chest toward the right; gaze up past the right shoulder.
Switch sides.
benefits: Strengthens and stretches the legs, ankles, and groin
Improves digestion and detoxifies the abdomen

instructions: Stand in Mountain Pose with feet hip-width apart.
Exhale and hinge forward from the hips, lengthening the spine as you fold.
Bend the knees as much as needed to release the lower back.
Let the head and neck hang heavy; bring fingertips to the floor, shins, or grip opposite elbows.
Stay for several breaths.
benefits: Calms the nervous system and relieves stress
contraindications: Lower back injury (keep knees bent or fold halfway)

instructions: From Standing Forward Fold, press fingertips into the floor or shins.
Inhale and lengthen the spine forward, parallel to the floor.
Draw the shoulders down the back; lift the chest forward.
Gaze a few inches in front of the mat.
benefits: Prepares the body for deeper folds and Chaturanga

instructions: Step the feet 3.5 to 4 feet apart, toes turned slightly in.
Place hands on the hips and lift the chest.
Exhale and hinge forward from the hips, bringing hands to the floor under the shoulders.
Bend the elbows back and let the crown of the head reach toward the floor.
Press the outer edges of the feet down.
benefits: Stretches the inner thighs, hamstrings, and spine

instructions: Step the left foot back 3 feet, back foot angled 45 degrees, hips squared forward.
Place hands on the hips and lengthen the spine.
Exhale and hinge forward over the front leg, hands to blocks or the floor on either side of the front foot.
Keep both legs straight and active; release the crown toward the shin.
Switch sides.
contraindications: Hamstring injury (keep a generous bend in the front knee)

instructions: Lie face down with legs extended and tops of the feet pressing into the mat.
Place palms under the shoulders, elbows hugged into the ribs.
Inhale and press the chest forward and up, keeping the lower ribs on the mat.
Draw the shoulders down the back; lengthen the back of the neck.
Use very little hand pressure; let the back muscles do the work.
benefits: Strengthens the spine and back muscles

instructions: From Chaturanga, untuck the toes so the tops of the feet press into the mat.
Straighten the arms and roll over the toes, lifting the chest forward and up.
Press the thighs and knees off the mat by engaging the legs.
Roll shoulders back; lengthen the tailbone toward the heels.
Gaze forward, jaw soft.
benefits: Stretches chest, lungs, shoulders, and abdomen

instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat hip-width apart, heels close to the sit bones.
Press into the feet and lift the hips toward the ceiling.
Interlace the hands under the back; roll the shoulders under.
Lift the chest toward the chin and keep the chin lifted slightly off the chest.
Hold, then release slowly, vertebra by vertebra.
benefits: Stretches chest, neck, spine, and hips
Calms the brain and reduces anxiety
contraindications: Lower back pain (use a block under the sacrum)

instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat and hip-width apart, close to the sit bones.
Bend the elbows and place the palms on the mat beside the ears, fingers pointing toward the shoulders.
Press into the hands and feet to lift the hips and crown of the head off the floor.
Straighten the arms as much as possible; lift the chest toward the back wall.
Lower slowly, tucking the chin to release.
benefits: Strengthens arms, wrists, legs, glutes, and spine
Opens chest, shoulders, and hip flexors deeply
contraindications: Headache, heart problems, or high blood pressure

instructions: Kneel with knees hip-width apart, shins and tops of feet pressing into the mat.
Place hands on the lower back, fingers pointing down, and lift the chest.
Begin to arch back; if accessible, reach the hands one at a time to the heels.
Press the hips forward over the knees; keep the neck long.
To exit, bring hands to the lower back and lift up slowly.
benefits: Stretches the entire front of the body
contraindications: Serious low back or neck injury

instructions: Lie face down with arms by your sides.
Bend the knees and reach back to grasp the outsides of the ankles.
Inhale and press the feet into the hands to lift the chest and thighs off the floor.
Draw the shoulders back; gaze forward.
Breathe steadily and release gently.
benefits: Stretches the entire front of the body, chest, and hip flexors

instructions: Lie face down with arms by the sides, palms up, forehead on the mat.
Inhale and lift the head, chest, arms, and legs off the floor.
Reach the fingertips back toward the feet; keep the gaze low to protect the neck.
Press the pubic bone into the mat; engage the glutes lightly.
Hold for several breaths and release.
benefits: Strengthens the back of the body — spine, glutes, hamstrings

instructions: Start on hands and knees with wrists under shoulders, knees under hips.
Tuck the toes and lift the hips up and back, forming an inverted V.
Press the floor away through the palms; spread the fingers wide.
Send the heels toward the floor (they don't need to touch).
Let the head hang between the arms; gaze toward the navel.
benefits: Stretches hamstrings, calves, shoulders, and hands
Calms the nervous system and energizes the body
contraindications: High blood pressure (modify with head supported on a block)

instructions: From hands and knees, lower the forearms to the mat with elbows shoulder-width apart.
Interlace the fingers or keep palms flat, parallel.
Tuck the toes and lift the hips up and back, like Downward Dog on the forearms.
Press the forearms firmly down and lift the shoulders away from the ears.
Walk the feet in slightly to deepen the stretch.
Kneel and interlace the fingers, creating a basket on the mat with elbows shoulder-width apart.
Place the crown of the head on the mat, cradled by the hands.
Tuck the toes and lift the hips, walking the feet in.
Slowly lift one knee, then the other, to the chest; carefully extend the legs straight up.
Press the forearms down to lift weight off the head; engage the core.
benefits: Said to calm the mind and reduce stress

instructions: Lie on your back; lift the legs straight up to 90 degrees.
Press the palms into the floor and swing the legs overhead, hips off the mat.
Support the back with the hands, elbows shoulder-width apart on the mat.
Lift the legs straight up toward the ceiling, weight resting on shoulders, not the neck.
Lower slowly with control.
benefits: Calms the brain and helps relieve stress
Stimulates the thyroid and abdominal organs

instructions: Sit sideways against a wall; swing the legs up the wall as you lie back.
Scoot the sit bones as close to the wall as is comfortable.
Rest the arms by the sides, palms up.
Soften the face and breathe; remain for 5 to 15 minutes.
To exit, bend the knees, roll to one side, and rest before sitting up.
benefits: Gently stretches the hamstrings and back of the neck

instructions: Stand in Mountain Pose. Shift weight into the left foot.
Bend the right knee and place the sole of the right foot on the inner left calf or thigh (avoid the knee).
Press the foot and inner leg into each other.
Bring hands to heart center or sweep them overhead.
Fix the gaze on a steady point. Switch sides.
benefits: Strengthens ankles, calves, thighs, and spine

instructions: From Triangle on the right side, bend the right knee and walk the right hand forward about a foot.
Shift weight into the right foot and right hand (or a block) as you lift the left leg parallel to the floor.
Straighten the standing leg; flex the lifted foot, top hip stacked over the bottom.
Extend the left arm to the ceiling; gaze up if balance allows.
Switch sides.
benefits: Strengthens ankles, thighs, and abdominal muscles
Stretches the groin, hamstrings, calves, and chest
contraindications: Neck issues (look forward instead of up)

instructions: Stand in Mountain Pose; bend both knees slightly.
Lift the right leg and cross it over the left thigh; hook the right foot behind the left calf if possible.
Cross the left arm under the right at the elbows; wrap forearms so palms meet.
Lift the elbows to shoulder height; press palms together.
Sit deeper into the standing leg. Switch sides.
benefits: Strengthens and stretches the ankles, calves, thighs, hips, and shoulders

instructions: Stand in Mountain Pose; shift weight into the left foot.
Bend the right knee and reach back with the right hand to grasp the inside of the right foot or ankle.
Extend the left arm forward at shoulder height for a focal point.
Begin to kick the right foot back and up, hinging the torso forward.
Maintain a strong standing leg and steady breath. Switch sides.
benefits: Stretches shoulders, chest, thighs, groin, and abdomen

instructions: Squat with feet together, knees wide.
Place palms shoulder-width apart on the mat in front of you.
Lift the hips, bend the elbows, and place the knees high on the backs of the upper arms.
Shift weight forward into the hands; lift one foot at a time, then both.
Gaze slightly forward; engage the core.
benefits: Strengthens arms, wrists, and abdominal muscles
Tones the abdomen and improves digestion

instructions: From Plank Pose, shift weight onto the right hand and outer edge of the right foot.
Stack the left foot on top of the right; lift the hips so the body forms a long diagonal.
Extend the left arm to the ceiling; gaze up if comfortable.
Engage the core and press the bottom hand strongly into the mat.
Switch sides.
benefits: Strengthens arms, core, wrists, and legs

instructions: Stand in Mountain Pose. Shift weight into the left foot.
Bend the right knee and hook the first two fingers around the right big toe (use a strap if needed).
Extend the right leg forward, straightening as much as possible.
Left hand on hip; stand tall.
For the deeper variation, open the leg out to the right side. Switch sides.
Sit on the mat with legs crossed at the shins, each foot under the opposite knee.
Sit on a folded blanket or block to lift the hips if the lower back rounds.
Rest the hands on the knees or in the lap.
Lengthen the spine; relax the shoulders down.
Breathe softly; close the eyes if it feels right.
benefits: Opens the hips and lengthens the spine
Calms the mind for meditation or pranayama
contraindications: Knee or hip injury (sit on a cushion or in a chair)

instructions: Sit with legs extended straight in front of you, feet flexed.
Press the palms into the mat beside the hips.
Lengthen the spine; engage the thigh muscles.
Lift through the crown of the head; relax the shoulders down.
Hold and breathe.
benefits: Strengthens the back muscles and core
contraindications: Lower back pain (sit on a folded blanket)

instructions: Kneel with knees together and feet slightly wider than hips, tops of the feet flat on the mat.
Lower the hips between the heels; use a block or folded blanket under the sit bones if needed.
Rest the hands on the thighs, palms down.
Lengthen the spine; soften the shoulders.
Hold for as long as is comfortable.
benefits: Stretches the thighs, knees, and ankles

instructions: Sit with the spine long; bend the knees and bring the soles of the feet together.
Let the knees fall out to the sides; clasp the feet or ankles.
Sit tall; gently encourage the knees toward the floor without forcing.
Optional: hinge forward from the hips for a deeper stretch.
Release slowly.
benefits: Opens the hips and inner thighs
Stimulates abdominal organs and pelvic circulation
contraindications: Groin or knee injury (support knees with blocks or blankets)

instructions: Sit in Staff Pose with legs extended.
Inhale and lift the arms overhead; lengthen the spine.
Exhale and hinge forward from the hips, reaching for the shins, ankles, or feet.
Keep the spine long; bend the knees slightly if the back rounds.
Let the head and neck soften.
benefits: Stretches the spine, hamstrings, and shoulders
contraindications: Lower back injury (keep a generous bend in the knees)

instructions: From Staff Pose, bend the right knee and step the right foot outside the left thigh.
Bend the left knee and tuck the left foot beside the right hip (or keep the left leg extended).
Inhale, lengthen the spine; exhale and twist to the right, left elbow outside the right knee.
Right hand on the mat behind you; gaze over the right shoulder.
Switch sides.
Step the left foot back about 3 feet, back foot angled in, hips squared forward.
Hinge forward over the front leg and place the left hand on the floor or a block outside the right foot.
Open the chest to the right; extend the right arm to the ceiling.
Press both legs straight and active; gaze up if comfortable.
Switch sides.
From Staff Pose, bend the right knee and place the right foot flat on the mat close to the sit bones.
Press the left leg active and long.
Inhale and lengthen; exhale and twist to the right, hooking the left elbow outside the right knee.
Right hand on the floor behind you.
Switch sides.
benefits: Releases tension in the spine and shoulders

instructions: Lie on your back. Hug the right knee into the chest.
Cross the right knee over to the left side of the body.
Extend the right arm out to the right at shoulder height; gaze over the right hand.
Press the right shoulder toward the floor; breathe into the twist.
Switch sides.
benefits: Stretches the chest, shoulders, and outer hips

instructions: From Chair Pose, bring the palms together at heart center.
Inhale to lengthen; exhale and twist to the right, hooking the left elbow outside the right thigh.
Press the palms firmly together; keep the knees even with each other.
Open the chest to the right; gaze up if your neck allows.
Switch sides.
From Downward Dog, bring the right knee forward toward the right wrist; shin angled toward the left wrist.
Extend the left leg straight back, top of the foot on the mat, hips squared.
Use a folded blanket under the right hip if it's lifted.
Stay upright on the fingertips, or fold forward over the front leg for a deeper release.
Switch sides.
benefits: Deeply opens the hips and glutes
Stretches the hip flexors of the back leg
contraindications: Knee injury (skip this pose or try Figure 4 on the back)

instructions: From Downward Dog, step the right foot to the outside of the right hand.
Lower the left knee to the mat (or keep it lifted for a deeper variation).
Walk the right foot wider and angle the toes slightly out.
Lower to the forearms onto a block or the mat.
Stay and breathe; switch sides.
benefits: Stretches the hips, hamstrings, and hip flexors
contraindications: Tight hips (work on the forearms on blocks)

instructions: Sit with legs extended. Bend the right knee under the left, right heel beside the left hip.
Cross the left leg over the right, stacking the knees, left heel beside the right hip.
Reach the right arm up and bend the elbow down behind the head.
Reach the left arm down and bend it up behind the back; clasp the hands or use a strap.
Sit tall and breathe. Switch sides.
benefits: Stretches ankles, hips, thighs, shoulders, armpits, and triceps

instructions: Lie on your back. Hug the knees into the chest.
Catch the outsides of the feet with the hands (or grab the big toes, or use a strap).
Open the knees wider than the torso, drawing them toward the armpits.
Keep the ankles directly over the knees; flex the feet.
Gently rock side to side if it feels good.
benefits: Opens the hips and inner thighs
Calms the brain and relieves stress

instructions: Stand with feet slightly wider than hip-width, toes turned out about 45 degrees.
Bend the knees deeply and lower the hips into a squat.
Bring the palms together at heart center; press the elbows lightly into the inner knees to open the hips.
Lift the chest; lengthen the spine.
Place a block under the sit bones if heels lift off the mat.
benefits: Opens the hips, groin, and ankles
contraindications: Knee, lower back, or ankle injury

instructions: From hands and knees, step the feet back so the body forms one long line.
Stack the shoulders directly over the wrists; spread the fingers wide.
Engage the core; press the heels back as you reach the crown forward.
Avoid lifting the hips or letting them drop.
Breathe and hold.
benefits: Strengthens the arms, wrists, and core
contraindications: Wrist injury or carpal tunnel (try forearm plank)

instructions: From Plank Pose, shift forward slightly onto the balls of the feet.
Hug the elbows in close to the ribs as you lower the body halfway down.
Stop when the elbows form a 90-degree angle, shoulders even with the elbows.
Keep the body in one long line; engage the legs and core.
Modify by lowering the knees first if needed.
benefits: Strengthens the arms, wrists, and core
Prepares the body for arm balances

instructions: Sit with knees bent, feet on the floor.
Lean back slightly and lift the feet, bringing the shins parallel to the floor.
Extend the arms forward, parallel to the floor at shoulder height.
Optional: straighten the legs so the body forms a V.
Engage the core; keep the spine long, not rounded.
benefits: Strengthens the abdomen, hip flexors, and spine
Stimulates the kidneys, thyroid, and intestines

instructions: Kneel on the mat with big toes touching and knees apart (or together).
Sit the hips back toward the heels.
Walk the hands forward and lower the forehead toward the mat.
Rest the arms extended forward or alongside the body.
Breathe deeply; rest as long as you need.
benefits: Releases the back, shoulders, and neck
Calms the mind and reduces stress
Gently stretches hips, thighs, and ankles
contraindications: Knee injury (place a blanket behind the knees)
Pregnancy (keep knees wide, no compression on the belly)

instructions: Lie flat on your back with legs slightly apart, feet relaxed open.
Rest the arms by the sides, a few inches away from the body, palms up.
Close the eyes; soften the face, jaw, tongue, and breath.
Allow the body to be completely still and heavy.
Rest for at least 5 minutes.
benefits: Lowers blood pressure and reduces anxiety
contraindications: Pregnancy (lie on the left side with support)
Back pain (place a bolster under the knees)

instructions: Lie on your back. Bend the knees and bring the soles of the feet together.
Let the knees fall open to the sides; support them with blocks or blankets.
Rest one hand on the belly and one on the heart, or open the arms wide.
Soften the body completely.
Stay for 5 to 10 minutes.
benefits: Eases menstrual discomfort and mild depression
contraindications: Groin or knee injury (use ample support under knees)
Lower back issues (place a bolster under the spine)

instructions: Start on hands and knees, wrists under shoulders, knees under hips.
Exhale and round the spine toward the ceiling; tuck the tailbone.
Drop the head toward the chest.
Pair with Cow Pose, flowing breath to movement.
Repeat for 5 to 10 rounds.
benefits: Stretches the back, torso, and neck
Gently massages the spine and abdominal organs
Releases tension between the shoulder blades
contraindications: Wrist injury (come onto forearms or fists)
Neck injury (keep the head in line with the spine)

instructions: Start on hands and knees, wrists under shoulders, knees under hips.
Inhale and drop the belly toward the mat as you lift the chest and tailbone.
Soften the shoulders down the back; gaze gently forward and up.
Pair with Cat Pose, linking breath to movement.
Repeat for 5 to 10 rounds.
contraindications: Wrist pain (come onto forearms or fists)

instructions: Kneel with knees together, tops of the feet flat on the mat, big toes touching.
Sit back on the heels.
Rest the hands on the thighs, palms down.
Lengthen the spine; relax the shoulders down.
Breathe steadily; place a folded blanket between the calves and thighs if needed.
From a standing forward fold, place the left fingertips on the floor or a block in front of the right foot.
Lift the left leg straight back, parallel to the floor, foot flexed.
Twist the chest open to the right; reach the right hand to the ceiling.
Stack the hips; gaze up if your neck allows.
Switch sides.
benefits: Strengthens the legs, ankles, and core

instructions: Sit with legs wider than hip-width, knees bent, feet on the mat.
Slide each arm under the corresponding knee, palms down.
Lower the chest toward the mat between the legs.
Straighten the legs as much as is comfortable, drawing the heels forward.
Soften the head and rest deeply.
benefits: Deeply stretches the back, hips, and inner thighs
contraindications: Lower back, shoulder, or hamstring injury

instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart. Fold forward and walk the hands behind the feet.
Bend the knees and work the shoulders behind the thighs, palms flat on the mat.
Sit the hips back onto the upper arms; press the hands firmly down.
Shift weight into the hands and extend the legs out to the sides, parallel to the floor.
Engage the core; flex the feet.
benefits: Strengthens the arms, wrists, and core
Stretches the inner thighs and back
contraindications: Wrist, elbow, shoulder, or lower back injury
